message:
chore: pin git deps to exact revs, format proptest, sync beads

Cargo.toml:
- Pin rich_rust and asupersync git dependencies to exact commit revs
  instead of tracking branch heads, preventing unexpected dependency
  drift across agent sessions

proptest_count_min_sketch.rs:
- Collapse multi-line CmsConfig construction to single line per rustfmt

.beads/issues.jsonl:
- Update ft-30fmc issue with IvoryEagle's progress comment

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-1ta8j","title":"Proactive pane lifecycle management — zombie reaping, stuck detection, age-based cleanup","description":"# Proactive Pane Lifecycle Management

## Skills: /extreme-software-optimization, /idea-wizard

## Problem
At 200+ panes, abandoned/stuck agents accumulate silently. A 54-hour agent consuming 118GB RAM was observed in production. Zombie processes consume process table slots. Stuck test processes (12+ hours) waste CPU.

## Solution: Activity-Based Lifecycle Engine

**NOTE**: This bead CONSUMES process_triage (wa-2zywl) as a library — it does NOT reimplement zombie reaping or stuck detection. process_triage provides the ProcessClassifier/TriageEngine/WhackAMoleDetector; this bead adds the FrankenTerm-specific health classification and pane-aware policies on top.

### Pane Health Classification (from /system-performance-remediation)
| Age | CPU% | Status | Action |
|-----|------|--------|--------|
| <4h | >10% | Active | Protect |
| <4h | <2% | Thinking | Protect |
| 4-16h | >5% | Working | Check children |
| 4-16h | <2% | Possibly stuck | Flag for review |
| 16-24h | any | Likely stuck | Kill if only MCP children |
| >24h | any | Abandoned | Kill immediately |

### Components to build

1. **PaneHealthMonitor**: Background task sampling per-pane CPU%, memory, child count, age
   - Sample every 30s (configurable)
   - Store in ring buffer for trend analysis
   - Expose via MCP tool and CLI
   - **macOS**: Use libproc/proc_pidinfo instead of /proc/[pid]/stat

2. **PaneTriagePolicy**: Wraps process_triage TriageEngine with pane-aware context
   - Maps pane health classification to process_triage kill hierarchy
   - Adds FrankenTerm-specific categories: "agent thinking" vs "agent stuck"
   - Per-pane policy overrides via configuration

3. **AgeReaper**: Configurable age-based cleanup policies
   - Default: warn at 16h, kill at 24h
   - Per-pane overrides via configuration
   - Graceful shutdown: SIGTERM, wait 30s, SIGKILL

4. **Resource pressure response**: When PSI avg10 > 30% (Linux) or CPU load > 80% (macOS)
   - Renice oldest idle panes to nice=19, ionice class 3 (idle)
   - Pause non-essential captures
   - Warn user via notification

## Formal requirement
The stuck detector whack-a-mole logic lives in process_triage (wa-2zywl) with formal verification. This bead only needs to verify that PaneTriagePolicy correctly maps pane context to process_triage categories.

## Tests
- Simulate 200 panes with various ages/CPU profiles, verify correct classification
- Test PaneTriagePolicy maps to process_triage categories correctly
- Test age reaper respects graceful shutdown
- Load test: 200 pane health checks in <100ms total
- **Use criterion benchmarks for health check latency**

## Acceptance criteria
- Health classification matches the table above
- Delegates zombie/stuck/whack-a-mole to process_triage library
- Age reaper respects graceful shutdown
- CPU pressure triggers renice within 5s
- Works on both Linux (/proc) and macOS (libproc)
- All under /extreme-software-optimization methodology: profile before/after","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:10:51.270912Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:08:24.676026Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T05:08:24.676026Z","close_reason":"Closed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1ta8j","depends_on_id":"ft-2zywl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1ta8j","depends_on_id":"ft-a0owq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"ft-1u55z","title":"Replace TCP networking and TLS (tokio-rustls → asupersync)","description":"# Replace TCP networking and TLS

## Background
TCP and TLS are used by feature-gated modules: web server (web.rs), distributed mode (distributed.rs), and HTTP clients (reqwest).

## Scope

### TCP (web.rs)
- TcpListener for the HTTP API server
- Bound to configurable port, serves /health, /panes, /events, /search, etc.
- Currently uses fastapi::TcpServer wrapper around tokio TcpListener

### TLS (distributed.rs)
- tokio-rustls 0.26 for mTLS between distributed nodes
- Certificate and private key loading
- TLS 1.2+ configuration
- Client and server TLS config

### HTTP Client (reqwest)
- reqwest 0.12.12 with rustls-tls feature
- Used for outbound API calls
- Must be replaced with asupersync::http client

## Migration
```rust
// TCP Before
use tokio::net::TcpListener;
let listener = TcpListener::bind(addr).await?;

// TCP After
use asupersync::net::TcpListener;
let listener = TcpListener::bind(cx, addr).await?;

// TLS Before
use tokio_rustls::TlsConnector;

// TLS After
use asupersync::tls::TlsConnector;  // rustls-based, no tokio dep

// HTTP Before
let resp = reqwest::get(url).await?;

// HTTP After
let client = asupersync::http::Client::new();
let resp = client.get(cx, url).await?;
```

## Note on FastAPI/FastMCP
These vendored frameworks are built on tokio. They will need either:
1. Porting to asupersync (significant effort)
2. Replacing with asupersync-native HTTP/MCP server (if available)
3. Running in a compatibility shim (last resort)

This bead covers the direct TCP/TLS replacement. FastAPI/FastMCP refactoring is tracked separately.

## Acceptance criteria
- TCP server binds and accepts connections via asupersync
- TLS handshake works with asupersync TLS
- HTTP client makes successful outbound requests
- reqwest dependency removed
- tokio-rustls dependency removed

## macOS platform note
- **kqueue for TCP readiness**: On macOS, asupersync uses kqueue (not epoll) for TCP socket readiness notification (accept, read, write events). Verify that asupersync's reactor correctly uses kqueue for TcpListener and TcpStream on Darwin targets. If any platform-specific code exists for TCP handling, ensure `#[cfg(target_os = "macos")]` paths use kqueue and `#[cfg(target_os = "linux")]` paths use epoll. This is especially important for the web server's accept loop and the distributed module's TLS connections.

## Benchmark requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks for TLS handshake and throughput**: Add `benches/tcp_tls.rs` measuring:
  - TCP connection establishment latency (bind + connect + accept)
  - TLS handshake latency (client hello → finished) for TLS 1.2 and TLS 1.3
  - mTLS handshake latency (mutual certificate verification)
  - Sustained TCP throughput (MB/s) for streaming data over plain TCP
  - Sustained TLS throughput (MB/s) for streaming data over TLS
  - Comparison with tokio-rustls for equivalent TLS operations

## LabRuntime DPOR
- **Concurrent connection testing with DPOR**: Use LabRuntime schedule exploration (DPOR) to test concurrent TCP/TLS scenarios:
  - Multiple clients connecting to TcpListener simultaneously — verify all connections accepted, no drops
  - Interleaved TLS handshakes — verify no state corruption between concurrent handshakes
  - Race between connection accept and listener shutdown — verify clean teardown
  - Concurrent reads and writes on established TLS connections — verify no data corruption or reordering","notes":"Progress 2026-02-14 (MistyCompass): completed reqwest->asupersync webhook HTTP migration in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs using asupersync::http::h1::HttpClient; removed direct reqwest dependency from crates/frankenterm/Cargo.toml and workspace reqwest/tokio-rustls entries from Cargo.toml; distributed TLS/TCP paths already migrated earlier to asupersync net/tls across main.rs + distributed.rs/tests. Validation: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-misty-wa-1u55z cargo check -p frankenterm --all-targets ✅; cargo check -p frankenterm --all-targets --features distributed ✅; cargo test -p frankenterm-core --features distributed --test distributed_security_e2e ✅ (4/4). fmt/clippy remain blocked by pre-existing unrelated workspace drift/lint debt.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CyanWolf","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:49:05.178507Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-16T00:34:10.093577Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1u55z","depends_on_id":"ft-1yz79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u55z","depends_on_id":"ft-2abzy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Validation: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-misty-wa-1u55z cargo check -p frankenterm --all-targets ✅; cargo check -p frankenterm --all-targets --features distributed ✅; cargo test -p frankenterm-core --features distributed --test distributed_security_e2e ✅ (4/4). fmt/clippy remain blocked by pre-existing unrelated workspace drift/lint debt.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CyanWolf","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:49:05.178507Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-16T00:49:20.191850Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1u55z","depends_on_id":"ft-1yz79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u55z","depends_on_id":"ft-2abzy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":344,"issue_id":"ft-1u55z","author":"CyanWolf","text":"Stale-takeover validation status: (1) Grep shows no remaining occurrences of reqwest/tokio-rustls/tokio_rustls in workspace manifests (Cargo.toml, crates/frankenterm/Cargo.toml, crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml).  {"id":"ft-1u8k","title":"CLI: set pane priority at runtime","description":"## What
Add a command to set pane priority/weights without restarting.

## Why
Operators need to react to incidents in real time.

## How
- CLI: `wa panes priority <pane_id> --weight N`
- Persist override in storage or in-memory registry with TTL

## Success Criteria
- Priority changes take effect immediately
- Overrides are visible via status/health output","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:05:00.210578659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T06:36:32.039776Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T06:36:32.039780Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-1u90p","title":"[EPIC] Zero-Hitch Resize/Reflow Rewrite for WezTerm Mux (60+ FPS, crash-free, smart wrapping)","description":"## Context
FrankenTerm inherits severe resize/font-change instability and pathological latency from current WezTerm/mux resize behavior under large scrollback and multi-pane/tab concurrency. Observed failure modes include long stalls, visual artifacts (stretched text before settle), and occasional crashes.

## Objective
Redesign the full resize/reflow pipeline so user-perceived behavior is effectively instantaneous, artifact-free, and robust under heavy load. Target sustained smoothness at 60+ FPS where hardware allows, with graceful degradation and deterministic behavior when hardware is constrained.

## Product Goals
- Near-zero hitching on resize and font changes
- No stretched/garbled intermediate frames
- Stable behavior with huge scrollback and many panes/tabs
- Intelligent line-breaking quality for terminal text (monospace-adapted Knuth-Plass + pragmatic fallbacks)
- Full backwards compatibility with existing terminal semantics and safety constraints

## Engineering Goals
- Formalized latency budgets, frame budgets, and correctness invariants
- Coalesced/asynchronous resize control plane (latest intent wins)
- Incremental viewport-first reflow and background completion
- GPU-first rendering/presentation strategy with robust CPU fallback parity
- Comprehensive test matrix: unit, property, integration, e2e, perf, soak, failure-injection

## Non-Negotiables
- No feature regressions in terminal behavior
- No architecture that relies on best-effort timing heuristics alone
- No rollout without measurable pass/fail gates

## Deliverables
A complete multi-track implementation and validation program covering architecture, algorithms, rendering, mux concurrency, reliability hardening, and production rollout controls.

## Success Criteria
- All track epics complete with objective evidence and no unresolved high-risk blocker.
- SLO and quality gates are satisfied across correctness, latency, artifact suppression, and stability.
- Rollout readiness (flags, dashboards, runbooks, rollback) is demonstrated before go/no-go sign-off.","design":"Two-phase resize architecture (interactive + background completion), latest-intent coalescing, artifact-free atomic presentation, and GPU-first with deterministic CPU parity.","acceptance_criteria":"All track epics complete with objective SLO gates passed; full test matrix green; rollout package approved; final go/no-go evidence accepted.","notes":"Any major design change must include benchmark evidence and explicit regression-risk analysis in bead comments.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:24:22.052067Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T01:22:39.519394Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","gpu","mux","performance","reflow","resize","ux"],"comments":[{"id":54,"issue_id":"ft-1u90p","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"# Master Execution Blueprint: Zero-Hitch Resize/Reflow

## Problem statement (user-facing)
Current resize/font-change behavior under mux + large scrollback can exhibit:
- long interactive stalls
- visual corruption/transient stretched text
- lock-amplified latency across panes/tabs
- crash risk under resize storms

This epic exists to make resize behavior feel immediate and stable in real-world heavy workloads while preserving terminal correctness.

## Architectural thesis
1. **Control-plane first**: explicit resize transaction lifecycle with latest-intent semantics.
2. **Viewport-first convergence**: interactive phase prioritizes visible work under strict budget; background phase completes cold scrollback.
3. **Artifact-free presentation**: never present incoherent intermediate frames.
4. **Algorithmic quality under budget**: monospace-adapted Knuth-Plass where feasible, deterministic fallback when budget pressure rises.
5. **Reliability over heroics**: enforce invariants, detect stalls, degrade gracefully, and preserve interactivity.
6. **Evidence-driven rollout**: no release without objective SLO gates and reproducible validation artifacts.

## Global success metrics
- No catastrophic interaction stalls during resize/font churn
- No stretched-text artifact class in e2e validation
- Crash budget effectively zero for resize paths in soak + fault-injection runs
- Deterministic correctness for Unicode width/cursor invariants
- CI catches perf regressions before merge

## Dependency logic
- Baseline forensics (Track 1) must establish bottleneck truth before architectural commitments.
- Control plane (Track 2) enables safe orchestration for layout/render/mux tracks.
- Layout/render/mux tracks execute in parallel after control-plane foundations stabilize.
- Reliability and validation tracks formalize correctness and operational confidence.
- Rollout track operationalizes compatibility contracts, canaries, alerts, and rollback.

## Testing doctrine (non-negotiable)
- Unit + property + integration + e2e + perf + soak are required.
- Every test domain must emit machine-readable diagnostics.
- Performance gates must be enforced in CI with explicit thresholds.
- Regression triage must be possible from artifacts alone (no manual guesswork).

## Scope constraints
- No feature loss in terminal semantics.
- No hidden coupling that requires undocumented “timing luck”.
- No rollout based on anecdotal performance impressions.

## Notes for future implementers
- Preserve comparability: don’t change benchmark harnesses and thresholds silently.
- Every major optimization should carry before/after measurements and risk notes.
- If quality-vs-latency tradeoffs are introduced, document exact fallback criteria.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:28:44Z"},{"id":55,"issue_id":"ft-1u90p","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"# Plan-Space Optimization Review (v2)

Performed a full second-pass audit of all `wa-1u90p*` beads for:
- dependency topology and critical-path quality
- user-perceived responsiveness safeguards
- validation completeness (unit/integration/e2e/perf/soak)
- logging/artifact quality for rapid regression triage

## Key revisions applied
1. Added user-critical tasks for interaction continuity and perception:
- `wa-1u90p.2.7` input-first scheduler guardrails
- `wa-1u90p.7.8` input-latency e2e harness
- `wa-1u90p.7.9` automated visual artifact detector
- `wa-1u90p.7.7` alt-screen app conformance e2e
- `wa-1u90p.8.7` controlled beta feedback + telemetry loop

2. Added quality rigor tasks for layout/render adaptation:
- `wa-1u90p.3.7` wrap-quality corpus and scorecard
- `wa-1u90p.4.7` adaptive rendering quality policy by hardware tier

3. Tightened release gate dependencies:
- Final go/no-go (`wa-1u90p.8.6`) now explicitly depends on new high-signal e2e/perception tasks.

4. Strengthened critical gate descriptions:
- `wa-1u90p.1.4`, `wa-1u90p.2.1`, `wa-1u90p.7.4`, `wa-1u90p.8.1`, `wa-1u90p.8.6` now have explicit measurable acceptance definitions.

5. Improved validation observability requirements:
- Validation tasks now require structured correlation IDs, per-stage latency fields, and attached machine-readable evidence artifacts.

## Current quality status
- `br lint` on `wa-1u90p*`: 0 warnings
- cycle check on `wa-1u90p*`: 0 cycles

This revision preserves original scope/functionality and increases user-outcome confidence before implementation.

 {"id":"ft-1u90p.1","title":"Baseline Forensics, SLOs, and Bottleneck Attribution","description":"Establish reproducible worst-case workloads, hard performance SLOs, and evidence-based bottleneck attribution before deeper architectural changes.

## Success Criteria
- Child tasks for this track are completed or explicitly deferred with rationale.
- Track-level objectives are met with measurable evidence (correctness + performance where applicable).
- Validation artifacts and operational notes are captured for future maintainers.","design":"Baseline-first evidence track","acceptance_criteria":"All baseline artifacts complete and approved","notes":"Downstream tracks depend on this evidence","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"GentleBrook","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:24:22.159442Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-14T06:57:45.502972Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T06:57:45.502957Z","close_reason":"LavenderCastle: All 6 subtasks closed (1.1-1.6). Baseline forensics complete: 4 canonical scenarios reproduced, end-to-end timeline instrumented with 5 stages, lock/memory profiling done with active-pane telemetry, SLOs defined (M1-M4), bottleneck dossier finalized with ranked interventions, frankentui component matrix audited.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","performance","reflow","resize","track-baseline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.1","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":56,"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"# Track 1 Detailed Intent: Baseline Forensics and SLO Grounding

This track creates the empirical foundation for the entire program.

## Why this track exists
Without reproducible scenarios and stage-level attribution, optimization effort drifts into guesswork. This track forces a measurable starting point.

## Required outputs
- deterministic stress scenarios
- resize timeline instrumentation (with queue/phase visibility)
- lock/memory profiling under storm load
- SLO/quality-gate document
- ranked bottleneck dossier
- frankentui reuse matrix (Knuth-Plass, scratch buffers, virtualization ideas)

## What “done” means
Any downstream implementation task should be able to point to this track’s artifacts and justify why it is expected to move specific metrics.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:28:44Z"},{"id":57,"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"GentleBrook progress update: hardened baseline docs for Track 1 with concrete instrumentation/source mapping.  {"id":"ft-2zywl","title":"Build and integrate process_triage — automated swarm process management","description":"# Build and Integrate process_triage

## Skills: /alien-artifact-coding (safety-critical), /extreme-software-optimization, /idea-wizard

## What process_triage should do
Automated, policy-driven process management for agent swarms. The system-performance-remediation skill's kill hierarchy, codified as a Rust library and deeply integrated into FrankenTerm.

## If process_triage already exists in /dp
Import as library dependency and integrate. If not, build it from the remediation skill's patterns.

## Kill hierarchy (from /system-performance-remediation)
| Priority | Category | Detection | Action | Risk |
|----------|----------|-----------|--------|------|
| 1 | Zombies | Z state | SIGCHLD to parent | Zero |
| 2 | Stuck tests | cargo test 12+ hours, <1% CPU | SIGTERM → SIGKILL | Low |
| 3 | Stuck CLIs | git/vercel 5+ min | SIGTERM | Low |
| 4 | Duplicate builds | Multiple cargo targets | Kill older, keep newest | Low |
| 5 | Abandoned servers | next dev/bun 24+ hours idle | SIGTERM | Low |
| 6 | Stale sessions | test tmux sessions | Direct kill | Medium |
| 7 | Confused agents | claude/codex 16+ hours | Kill parent, not children | Medium |
| 8 | Active agents | <16 hours, working | Protect or renice | High |
| 9 | System processes | systemd, sshd, postgres | NEVER TOUCH | Forbidden |

## Components

### 1. ProcessClassifier
Classify every process in the pane's process tree:
- PID, PPID, age, CPU%, memory, command, state
- Map to kill hierarchy category
- Output: ordered list of actions

### 2. TriageEngine
Execute actions in priority order:
- Dry-run mode: log what would happen
- Interactive mode: human approves each action
- Auto mode: execute non-destructive actions (zombies, stuck tests) automatically, queue medium-risk for approval

### 3. WhackAMoleDetector
Detect and break respawn cycles:
- Track kill→respawn patterns per process tree
- If child respawns within 5s of kill 3 times: kill the parent agent
- Formally verify this logic (/alien-artifact-coding)

### 4. Integration with FrankenTerm
- Runs as background task within FrankenTerm
- Pane-aware: knows which processes belong to which pane
- Triggers automatically on CPU pressure > 30%
- Manual trigger via CLI: wa triage [--dry-run]
- MCP tool: agents can request triage of their own pane

## Formal requirements (/alien-artifact-coding)
- ProcessClassifier MUST never misclassify a system process as killable
- TriageEngine MUST respect the priority ordering (never kill category 8 before category 2)
- WhackAMoleDetector MUST not false-positive on intentional restarts

## Tests
- Test classification of 20+ process scenarios
- Test kill hierarchy ordering
- Test whack-a-mole detection with mock process trees
- Test system process protection (NEVER kill sshd, mux server)
- Chaos test: random process creation/death, verify correct triage

## Acceptance criteria
- Process classification matches hierarchy table
- Triage executes in correct priority order
- Whack-a-mole detected and broken
- System processes NEVER touched
- Formally verified safety properties
- <500ms full triage scan for 2000+ processes

## macOS Compatibility
On macOS, `/proc` is not available for process enumeration. Use platform-specific alternatives:
- **Process enumeration**: `ps -o pid,ppid,args,state,%cpu,%mem,etime` instead of reading `/proc/[pid]/stat` and `/proc/[pid]/cmdline`
- **Process tree discovery**: `proc_pidinfo()` / `libproc.h` APIs (via the `libproc` crate) for per-process details, or fall back to `sysctl` KERN_PROC for the full process table
- **Zombie detection**: `ps -o state` reports `Z` on both platforms; no `/proc` needed
- **CPU/memory usage**: `proc_pid_rusage()` on macOS vs `/proc/[pid]/stat` on Linux
- Gate platform-specific code behind `#[cfg(target_os = "...")]` with a shared `ProcessInfo` abstraction

## Test Framework Requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks**: Benchmark triage decision latency — full classification of 2000 processes must complete in <500ms. Benchmark individual ProcessClassifier.classify() calls targeting <50μs per process. Include benchmark groups for varying process counts (100, 500, 1000, 2000).
- **Proptest**: Property-based tests for kill hierarchy correctness:
  - For any random set of processes, triage MUST process them in priority order (category 1 before 2, 2 before 3, etc.)
  - For any random process tree, system processes (category 9) are NEVER in the kill list
  - For any random kill/respawn sequence, WhackAMoleDetector correctly identifies respawn cycles (3+ respawns within 15s)

## Cross-References
- **wa-1ta8j** (Proactive pane lifecycle): process_triage provides the kill hierarchy that pane lifecycle management uses to decide when to reap zombies, detect stuck processes, and perform age-based cleanup. The two systems share process classification data.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:13:34.175116Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-11T04:28:07.883791Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T04:28:07.883791Z","close_reason":"Process triage engine implemented: TriageCategory enum (9 categories), ProcessClassifier with kill hierarchy, TriageEngine with plan builder, integration with process_tree module. 25 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-308u","title":"FTUI-07.3.b PTY failure artifact schema and triage playbook","description":"## Background
PTY failures are expensive without standardized artifacts and triage steps.

## Deliverables
- schema for required PTY failure artifacts (transcripts, environment, scenario metadata, screenshots/snapshots if applicable)
- step-by-step triage playbook from failure detection to root-cause classification
- concise failure summary format for CI and local runs

## Acceptance Criteria
- schema captures enough context to reproduce failures without ad-hoc digging
- playbook defines severity classes and escalation criteria
- CI output includes links/paths to all required artifacts.,","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:39.954101085Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:05:50.780303401Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:05:50.780171115Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-308u","depends_on_id":"ft-1qr1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-308u","depends_on_id":"ft-3gii","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
